# Tene

> Local-first encrypted secret management CLI that Claude Code understands.

## Overview

Tene is an open-source, local-first secret management tool built in Go, designed for developers working with Claude Code. It encrypts API keys, tokens, and credentials in a local SQLite vault using XChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption. Single binary, no runtime needed, no server, no signup, no cloud dependency.

## Key Features

- **Claude Code Auto-Detection**: `tene init` generates CLAUDE.md so Claude Code automatically knows how to retrieve secrets.
- **Local-First Encryption**: Secrets are encrypted with XChaCha20-Poly1305 (256-bit keys) and stored in a local SQLite vault.
- **Zero Network Calls**: 100% offline. No server, no telemetry, no internet requirement.
- **One Command Injection**: `tene run -- <command>` injects all secrets as environment variables.
- **Environment Switching**: `tene env prod` switches between development, staging, and production secrets.
- **.env Migration**: `tene import .env` migrates existing environment variables into the encrypted vault.
- **Single Binary**: Built in Go. No Node.js or other runtime required.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Install
go install github.com/tomo-kay/tene/cmd/tene@latest

# Or download from GitHub Releases
# https://github.com/tomo-kay/tene/releases

# Then
tene init
tene set STRIPE_KEY sk_test_xxx
tene run -- claude
```

## Encryption Architecture

- Master Password -> Argon2id (64MB memory, 3 iterations) -> Master Key (256-bit)
- Master Key cached in OS Keychain
- Each secret encrypted with XChaCha20-Poly1305 (unique 192-bit nonce)
- 12-word BIP-39 recovery key

## Tech Stack

- Language: Go
- CLI Framework: cobra
- Database: modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go, no CGo)
- Encryption: golang.org/x/crypto (XChaCha20-Poly1305 + Argon2id)
- Keychain: zalando/go-keyring
- Build: goreleaser (multi-platform binaries + Homebrew tap)

## Links

- Website: https://tene.dev
- GitHub: https://github.com/tomo-kay/tene
- License: MIT
- Price: Free (forever)

## Supported Platforms

- macOS (arm64, amd64)
- Linux (arm64, amd64)
- Windows (WSL)
